market_summary: U.S. equity futures are broadly higher today (S&P +0.52%, Nasdaq +0.72%) as the AI sector continues to process NVIDIA's blowout earnings from last week and macro optimism around Iran deal progress lifts sentiment; hedge funds are noted to be rotating into chip stocks while trimming software names.

MSFT.headline: MSFT trades modestly lower and below its 200-day moving average, though Wedbush raised its price target to $575 on the back of a restructured OpenAI deal that secures IP through 2032 and forecasts ~$6B in OpenAI-related revenue in 2026.
MSFT.reasoning: The stock is trading near the lower end of its 52-week range ($356.28–$555.45) after a broader software rotation, but the fundamentals remain intact — last quarter MSFT delivered EPS of $4.27 vs. $4.06 estimated (+5.18% surprise). The OpenAI deal restructuring (ending revenue-share, locking in IP access through 2032) reduces uncertainty and reinforces Azure's AI flywheel. Next earnings are July 28; no action needed for long-term holders.

AMZN.headline: Amazon trades flat near $268, with AWS cloud growing at its fastest pace in nearly four years and BofA pounding the table with a $310 price target, even as U.K. lawmakers hit the company with a new accusation under antitrust scrutiny.
AMZN.reasoning: AMZN is tracking close to its 52-week high of $278.56 (hit May 5) and net margins are running at 12.2% — matching a three-year peak that historically averaged just 6.6%, signaling a structural shift in the business. The U.K. regulatory headline is a watch item but not material given Amazon's global diversification and the earnings date of July 29 is the next key catalyst. Long-term thesis is intact.

NVDA.headline: NVDA rebounds ~1.2% in pre-market after last week's post-earnings dip, digesting a historic Q1 beat: $81.6B revenue (+85% YoY), Q2 guidance of $91B, an $80B buyback authorization, and a 2,400% dividend hike to $0.25/quarter.
NVDA.reasoning: This marks the fourth consecutive quarter NVDA beat-and-raised yet fell post-earnings — a 'sell the news' dynamic driven by a 20% pre-earnings run-up to an all-time high of $235.74, not fundamental weakness. Data center revenue doubled YoY to $75.2B (92% of sales), and CEO Jensen Huang stated demand has 'gone parabolic' with agentic AI arriving. With 37 analysts rating it a Buy and a consensus target of ~$297, the long-term AI infrastructure thesis is stronger than ever — the post-earnings pullback to ~$215 is noise, not signal.

GOOGL.headline: GOOGL rises modestly today but faces a potential record EU fine (high triple-digit million euros) over Digital Markets Act search practices, even as Google I/O's Gemini AI rollout across Search, Cloud, and Android earns renewed Buy reiterations from Goldman Sachs and Citi (PT $447).
GOOGL.reasoning: Alphabet started 2026 with 22% revenue growth to $110B and Google Cloud surging 63% to $20B, confirming its AI monetization thesis is materializing rapidly. The EU penalty, while headline-grabbing, is manageable relative to Alphabet's $422.5B TTM revenue and strong 37.9% net margin. WATCH status is warranted solely to monitor regulatory escalation; the core long-term thesis — dominant search, accelerating cloud, and deep Gemini integration — remains fully intact. Next earnings July 21.

alerts.message: EU reportedly preparing a high triple-digit million euro fine under the Digital Markets Act for Google search practices — monitor for formal ruling, but no action needed at current scale relative to revenues.

long_term_view: All four holdings — MSFT, AMZN, NVDA, GOOGL — remain at the structural center of the AI infrastructure and monetization supercycle, with each delivering accelerating revenue, expanding margins, and aggressive capital returns; today's modest price moves and one regulatory headline do not alter the multi-year thesis, and long-term holders should stay the course.
